notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET ‚Äî authored from the aider transport + the claudecode/codex/pi siblings of this scenario; not yet validated against a live recording (aider has NO provider auth in this environment ‚Äî headless aider hangs ~5min on the OpenRouter OAuth flow and the rig precheck delegates auth to the user ‚Äî so recording is deferred until auth is available). SCOPE_NOTE (agent_supports:partial): aider has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool. There is no tool-open/tool-result pair around the question, and the user-blocking-question/aider coverage cell (partial/no) is the verdict for that STRUCTURED-tool primitive, NOT for this cell. This cell exercises the adapter-agnostic trailing-? TEXT-classifier path (state_classifier.go decision 2a): the assistant's reply ends in a literal '?', so at turn end IsAgentDone() is true AND the last assistant message ends in '?', and the session transitions working‚ÜíWAITING instead of working‚Üíready. The load-bearing assertion is turn_end_waiting asserting expected_state:waiting. WHY THE CONTRACT HOLDS (irrlicht_observes:yes, flips the stale matrix 'no'): aider's parser buffers the assistant prose and, on the `> Tokens:` line, closeModelCall emits an assistant_message whose AssistantText preserves the trailing '?' (pinned by parser_test.go TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification); the tailer copies that non-empty text into session.LastAssistantText, which IsWaitingForUserInput inspects (core/domain/session/session.go, ExtractQuestionSnippet). The ~1500ms IdleFlush then synthesizes turn_done with EMPTY AssistantText (which does not overwrite the stored question) so the waiting classification survives. TRANSPORT: aider is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; there is no transcript-UUID rollout session ‚Äî the whole arc lives on the one proc-<PID> session row keyed by the daemon's process scanner (cf. basic-turn spec). RECORDED IN INTERACTIVE MODE (drive-aider-interactive.sh: tmux REPL), NOT headless `aider --message`: in headless mode aider answers and the process exits at turn end, so the working‚Üíwaiting episode is lost to a process_exited race (cf. codex/pi headless note). The interactive driver keeps the REPL alive after the turn ‚Äî torn down only at script end via tmux kill-session (no Ctrl-D / /exit) ‚Äî so the waiting state is observable and dwells; NO reply is sent to the question. SESSION-IDENTITY WORKAROUND (same as pi/multi-turn-conversation and the spec/SKILL.md proc-<PID> pitfall): the live REPL may surface a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, so session_birth ‚Äî which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' ‚Äî legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARC must land on the bindable session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never goes working); pid_bind, turn_end_waiting, waiting_dwell and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. TIMING: the working‚Üíwaiting settle lands a few seconds after the last transcript line ‚Äî gated on the ~1500ms IdleFlush turn_done clamped to the SessionDetector ~5s working-refresh tick ‚Äî so the trailing sleep must be ‚â•6s to let the waiting episode materialize before teardown; that is a latency delay, not a correctness gap, so the arc is still asserted. HOW RUNS DIFFER: exact assistant question wording, per-turn token counts, PID, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: one ready‚Üíworking‚ÜíWAITING arc on one proc-<PID> session that does NOT settle back to ready before teardown, a single process_exited, and a final waiting state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Prompt asking for one clarifying question ‚Üí working (binds the bindable session; a transient pre-session never enters working)

PID bound to the session (aider binds the PID at process birth, before the prompt is sent ‚Äî anchored to start so the launch-time pid_discovered is in window; same_session_as pins it to the bindable proc-<PID> row, never a transient pre-session)

The assistant reply ends in a question mark, so the turn settles working‚ÜíWAITING (the trailing-? text path, IsWaitingForUserInput ‚Üí rule 2a) rather than working‚Üíready ‚Äî the agent is waiting for the user to answer
No reply is sent; the session dwells in waiting ‚Äî the episode is visible and does not skip over or settle back to ready while the agent is gated on the user
The driver tears down the REPL (tmux kill-session) while the session is still waiting ‚Üí a single process_exited on the session
